title: DistroRun Configuration
description: Schema for DistroRun YAML configuration files. Defines the target OS, packages, users, services, and build options for a custom Linux image.

version.description: The version of your configuration file. Follows semantic versioning (e.g., "1", "1.0", "1.0.12").

name.description: The name of your DistroRun configuration.

distro.description: Defines the target operating system and its architecture profile. DistroRun automatically injects the necessary base packages (kernel, bootloader, init system) based on this selection.

type.description: The system profile. Required for Fedora (server or workstation). Ignored for Alpine.

packages.description: A list of software packages to install via the distribution's native package manager (dnf for Fedora, apk for Alpine). Do not include base system dependencies (like systemd or linux-kernel); the DistroRun engine handles these automatically.

users.description: Defines the system users. At least one user must be defined to ensure the system is accessible after booting.

password.description: The plain-text password. DistroRun reads this and securely hashes it (e.g., via SHA-512) before injecting it into /etc/shadow. The plain text is never stored in the final OS image.

services.description: Manages the runtime initialization of services. DistroRun translates these instructions into systemd symlinks for Fedora, or OpenRC runlevels for Alpine.

enable.description: Services to start automatically at boot.

build.description: Controls the behavior of the DistroRun Go engine during the artifact generation phase.

sbom.description: If true, the engine scans the final rootfs and generates a Software Bill of Materials (SPDX JSON). Set to false for faster, iterative development builds. Defaults to false if omitted.

output.description: Artifact format produced by the build. "iso" creates a bootable installer image (default). "disk" creates a qcow2 virtual disk for QEMU/libvirt. "img" creates a raw USB image with a persistence partition.

disk_size.description: Size of the virtual disk when output is "disk". Accepts suffixes like "G" or "M" (e.g., "4G", "8G", "512M"). Defaults to "4G". Ignored unless output is "disk".

persist_size.description: Size of the persistence partition when output is "img". Accepts suffixes like "G" or "M" (e.g., "2G", "512M"). Defaults to "2G". Ignored unless output is "img".
